heading: Public availability of information
label: 78x Public availability of information

children.heading: “Records” defined
children.label: a “Records” defined
children.text: For purposes of the term “records” includes all applications, statements, reports, contracts, correspondence, notices, and other documents filed with or otherwise obtained by the Commission pursuant to this chapter or otherwise.section 552 of title 5

children.heading: Disclosure or personal use
children.label: b Disclosure or personal use
children.text: It shall be unlawful for any member, officer, or employee of the Commission to disclose to any person other than a member, officer, or employee of the Commission, or to use for personal benefit, any information contained in any application, statement, report, contract, correspondence, notice, or other document filed with or otherwise obtained by the Commission (1) in contravention of the rules and regulations of the Commission under , or (2) in circumstances where the Commission has determined pursuant to such rules to accord confidential treatment to such information.section 552 of title 5

children.heading: Confidential disclosures
children.label: c Confidential disclosures
children.text: The Commission may, in its discretion and upon a showing that such information is needed, provide all “records” (as defined in subsection (a)) and other information in its possession to such persons, both domestic and foreign, as the Commission by rule deems appropriate if the person receiving such records or information provides such assurances of confidentiality as the Commission deems appropriate.

children.heading: Records obtained from foreign securities authorities
children.label: d Records obtained from foreign securities authorities
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (g), the Commission shall not be compelled to disclose records obtained from a foreign securities authority if (1) the foreign securities authority has in good faith determined and represented to the Commission that public disclosure of such records would violate the laws applicable to that foreign securities authority, and (2) the Commission obtains such records pursuant to (A) such procedure as the Commission may authorize for use in connection with the administration or enforcement of the securities laws, or (B) a memorandum of understanding. For purposes of , this subsection shall be considered a statute described in subsection (b)(3)(B) of such section 552.section 552 of title 5
the Commission is an agency responsible for the regulation or supervision of financial institutions; and
any entity for which the Commission is responsible for regulating, supervising, or examining under this chapter is a financial institution.

children.heading: Sharing privileged information with other authorities
children.label: f Sharing privileged information with other authorities

children.heading: Privileged information provided by the Commission
children.label: 1 Privileged information provided by the Commission
A
children.text: any agency (as defined in );section 6 of title 18

children.label: B
children.text: the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board;

children.label: C
children.text: any self-regulatory organization;

children.label: D
children.text: any foreign securities authority;

children.label: E
children.text: any foreign law enforcement authority; or

children.label: F
children.text: any State securities or law enforcement authority.

children.heading: Nondisclosure of privileged information provided to the Commission
children.label: 2 Nondisclosure of privileged information provided to the Commission
children.text: The Commission shall not be compelled to disclose privileged information obtained from any foreign securities authority, or foreign law enforcement authority, if the authority has in good faith determined and represented to the Commission that the information is privileged.

children.heading: Nonwaiver of privileged information provided to the Commission
children.label: 3 Nonwaiver of privileged information provided to the Commission

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Federal agencies, State securities and law enforcement authorities, self-regulatory organizations, and the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board shall not be deemed to have waived any privilege applicable to any information by transferring that information to or permitting that information to be used by the Commission.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The provisions of subparagraph (A) shall not apply to a self-regulatory organization or the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board with respect to information used by the Commission in an action against such organization.

children.label: A
children.text: the term “privilege” includes any work-product privilege, attorney-client privilege, governmental privilege, or other privilege recognized under Federal, State, or foreign law;

children.label: B
children.text: the term “foreign law enforcement authority” means any foreign authority that is empowered under foreign law to detect, investigate or prosecute potential violations of law; and

children.label: C
children.text: the term “State securities or law enforcement authority” means the authority of any State or territory that is empowered under State or territory law to detect, investigate, or prosecute potential violations of law.

alter the Commission’s responsibilities under the Right to Financial Privacy Act ( et seq.), as limited by , with respect to transfers of records covered by such statutes, or12 U.S.C. 3401section 78u(h) of this title

authorize the Commission to withhold information from the Congress or prevent the Commission from complying with an order of a court of the United States in an action commenced by the United States or the Commission.
